message:
The other day, I was trying to explain to my kids why having a go-bag ready is important. They were more interested in debating whether their favorite stuffed animals should come along or if we should pack extra snacks. I couldn’t help but chuckle at the sheer seriousness they brought to the discussion about gummy bears vs. granola bars! 

It made me realize that while I’m stressing about emergency plans, they’re just thinking about comfort and familiarity. So, we reached a compromise: a go-bag filled with essentials AND a few beloved toys. 

It's a sweet reminder that preparedness can be a family affair, and a little fun goes a long way in making serious topics more relatable. 

How do you involve your kids in family preparations? 

P.S. This little refresher on go-bags really helped us out!
